title: NodeListResponse
description: Response to GET /v1/discover. Returns nodes pre-scored and pre-sorted by the directory. See spec/iicp-dir.md §3.4 and iicp-semantics.md §3.

nodes.description: Ordered list of eligible nodes, highest score first. Empty array if no eligible nodes exist.

count.description: Number of nodes in this response.

query_ms.description: Directory query latency in milliseconds, when reported.

relay_available.description: True when at least one returned candidate advertises relay capability.

view.description: dispatch is route-bearing task-submission data; public is a presentation-safe redacted projection.

data_class.description: Machine-readable classification of whether route fields are present.

route_fields_present.description: True only when response nodes contain route-bearing fields such as endpoint or transport_endpoint.

redaction.description: Human/machine-readable summary of route-field redaction in the selected view.

dispatch_ticket_endpoint.description: Relative endpoint for explicit ticketed route-dispatch discovery.

ticketed_dispatch_available.description: True when the directory supports POST /v1/dispatch/ticket.

node_id.description: Globally unique node identifier assigned at registration. Present only in route-dispatch view.

node_id_prefix.description: Public presentation-safe node identifier prefix; full UUIDs are not exposed in view=public.

endpoint.description: Routable base URL for submitting tasks to this node. Present only in route-dispatch view. HTTPS is preferred and required for browser consumers from the HTTPS website; native/node consumers may receive routable http:// endpoints where allowed by iicp-core.md §8 and iicp-dir.md §3.1.

score.description: Directory-computed ADR-008 composite score. Nodes with score < 0.1 are excluded.

latency_estimate_ms.description: Estimated round-trip latency in milliseconds based on recent heartbeat timing. Optional; omit if unavailable.

available.description: True if node is within its availability window AND last_seen within 90 seconds.

cx_public_key.description: Canonical IICP-CX X25519 public key for encrypting task payloads to this node. Null means the node has not advertised CX support.

sdk_status.description: Directory-computed SDK posture against the current baseline.

sdk_baseline_version.description: SDK version baseline used for sdk_status.

upgrade_required.description: True when sdk_status is downlevel or unknown.

privacy_routing_status.description: Whether the node is fully CX-ready or still transitional/plaintext-capable only.

node_policy_manifest.description: Optional public node policy metadata derived from REGISTER policy_manifest. Unsigned manifests are self-attested; signed manifests carry directory-computed Ed25519 verification evidence. This is transparency/routing-policy evidence only; it is not a legal compliance certificate.

region.description: Region declared by the node at registration (e.g. eu-central). Optional.

transport_method.description: Reachability method mirrored from REGISTER. external_tunnel may be a named/operator-managed tunnel or an accountless temporary tunnel.

transport_candidates.description: ICE-style candidate list when advertised by the node.

reachability_tier.description: Directory routing tier: direct when dial-back/routability evidence exists, relay when reachable through a relay surface.

routing_hint.description: Directory/client-facing route preference hint, if emitted.

browser_usable.description: True when the endpoint/relay path is usable from HTTPS browser consumers.

route_evidence.description: Evidence labels supporting route usability, such as directory_observed or directory_probe.

input_modalities.description: Capability input modalities accepted for this node/intent; ["text"] is the default.

backend.description: Self-attested backend family such as ollama, lmstudio, vllm, llamacpp, anthropic or custom.

exposure_mode.description: ADR-043 network exposure classification when present.

public_reachable.description: Directory-observed public reachability flag when present.

models.description: Advisory model names surfaced by the directory.

capabilities.description: Capability subset relevant to the requested intent. Full capabilities available via GET /v1/node/{id}.

remote_executor_can_read_prompt.description: True for normal remote inference because the selected executor can read the prompt it executes.

training_use.description: Self-attested training/reuse posture. Unknown/absent values are to be treated conservatively as provider-defined.

signed_statement.description: Optional human-readable signed-policy reference. New implementations should prefer the structured verification block.

evidence.description: Evidence level for the manifest. Public NODELIST/DISCOVER normally expose self_attested or signed_verified because invalid/expired signed manifests are rejected at registration.

operator_governance.description: Safe known-operator governance evidence. Never includes raw operator keys, raw nonces, emails, identity documents, or private evidence references.

key.description: 32-byte X25519 public key, base64url-encoded without padding.
